full_title: Standards for Reporting of Diagnostic Accuracy Studies — Artificial Intelligence extension
source: Sounderajah V, Ashrafian H, Rose S, et al. Developing a reporting guideline for artificial intelligence-centred diagnostic test accuracy studies: the STARD-AI protocol. BMJ Open. 2021;11:e047709. EQUATOR Network guideline.
description: STARD-AI is a 18-item reporting guideline specifically for AI-based diagnostic test accuracy studies. Applicable when a model is used as a replacement or add-on to an existing diagnostic test (e.g., AI-based imaging diagnosis, AI-based ECG interpretation, AI-based biomarker-based screening). Not applicable to pure prognostic prediction models without a reference standard test.

use_when: AI model serves as a diagnostic test with a reference standard (e.g., pathology, culture, PCR)
Study design involves paired comparison of AI vs reference standard
Study reports sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV relative to a gold standard
AI model intended to replace or triage a conventional diagnostic test
not_applicable_when: Pure prognostic prediction (no reference standard test exists)
Risk stratification without diagnostic test context
applicability.note: When stard_ai.applicable = false, a justification must be provided in the checklist spec.

items.label: Title
items.text: Identify the study as involving an AI system for diagnosis or screening. Specify the index test (AI system), the reference standard, and the target condition.
items.pass_condition: Title explicitly identifies the AI index test and the target diagnostic condition

items.label: Abstract
items.text: Structured abstract stating the study design, participants, index test (AI system), reference standard, performance results (sensitivity, specificity, AUC) with confidence intervals, and conclusions.
items.pass_condition: Abstract contains structured elements: design, AI system description, reference standard, performance with CI

items.label: Scientific and Clinical Background
items.text: Describe the diagnostic condition, the burden of disease, existing diagnostic approaches, and the intended clinical role for the AI system (replacement, triage add-on, or rule-out).
items.pass_condition: Background describes the diagnostic gap the AI system fills and intended clinical use

items.label: Study Objectives
items.text: State the study objectives including the intended use of the AI index test, the reference standard used, and the study design (prospective, retrospective, single/multi-center).
items.pass_condition: Objectives explicitly state index test, reference standard, and study design

items.label: Data Collection
items.text: Describe the data collection process, including whether data was prospectively or retrospectively collected, dates of collection, and any linkage between data sources.
items.pass_condition: Data collection method, dates, and source described; prospective vs retrospective stated

items.label: Participants
items.text: Report the eligibility criteria for participants (inclusion/exclusion), the setting (primary care, hospital, screening program), and the clinical pathway for test ordering.
items.pass_condition: Eligibility criteria, setting, and recruitment pathway documented

items.label: Index Test and Reference Standard
items.text: Describe both the index test (AI system) and the reference standard in detail. For the AI system: algorithm type, training data source, software version. For the reference standard: method, qualification of those conducting it.
items.pass_condition: AI index test and reference standard both described with sufficient detail for reproducibility

items.label: AI System Technical Specifications
items.text: Report the AI system's technical specifications: architecture, training dataset demographics and size, any pre-training (transfer learning), software framework/version, and deployment environment.
items.pass_condition: Architecture, training data characteristics, software framework, and version all reported

items.label: AI Model Cutoffs and Thresholds
items.text: Describe the threshold(s) used to classify AI output as positive/negative, how thresholds were determined (pre-specified vs. data-driven), and on which data partition threshold selection was performed.
items.pass_condition: Classification threshold documented; confirmation that threshold was selected on validation (not test) data

items.label: Reference Standard
items.text: Describe the reference standard, including the methods used, the criteria for positivity, and whether the reference standard is likely to correctly classify the target condition.
items.pass_condition: Reference standard described with criteria for positivity and quality assessment

items.label: Blinding
items.text: State whether the AI system output was interpreted without knowledge of the reference standard result, and whether the reference standard was interpreted without knowledge of the AI output.
items.pass_condition: Blinding status of both AI interpretation and reference standard interpretation documented

items.label: Statistical Analysis
items.text: Describe the statistical methods used to estimate diagnostic accuracy (sensitivity, specificity, AUC, NPV, PPV), how confidence intervals were calculated, and how missing data and verification bias were handled.
items.pass_condition: Statistical methods described including CI computation method and missing data strategy

items.label: Subgroup and Sensitivity Analysis
items.text: Describe any planned subgroup analyses (by age, sex, disease severity, imaging vendor) or sensitivity analyses, including how the subgroups were defined.

items.label: Study Flow and Data
items.text: Report the number of participants enrolled, excluded (with reasons), and analyzed. Report the number with the target condition and without, and the number with indeterminate or missing results.
items.pass_condition: STARD flow diagram or equivalent table present with numbers per stage

items.label: Diagnostic Accuracy
items.text: Report the AI model's diagnostic accuracy including sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, and AUC, all with 95% confidence intervals. Present a cross-tabulation (confusion matrix) of AI results against the reference standard.
items.pass_condition: Sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, AUC all reported with 95% CI; confusion matrix present

items.label: AI-Specific Results
items.text: Report AI-specific results including: model calibration, subgroup performance analyses, failure mode analysis (types of false positives/negatives), and explainability outputs if used.
items.pass_condition: Calibration reported; subgroup results reported; at least qualitative failure analysis present

items.label: Limitations and Clinical Applicability
items.text: Discuss the study limitations including: spectrum bias, imperfect reference standard, potential for AI training-reference standard contamination, intended clinical use context, and conditions under which performance may degrade.
items.pass_condition: Limitations explicitly address spectrum bias, reference standard quality, and clinical applicability scope

items.label: Registration, Funding, and AI System Availability
items.text: Provide study registration information, funding sources, conflicts of interest, and information on AI system availability (commercial, open source, under development) including software version.
items.pass_condition: Funding, COI, and AI system availability (version and access) all documented
